question: Pure gauge compact U(1) lattice gauge theory (compact lattice QED) is simulated on four-dimensional hypercubic lattices with FIXED boundary conditions: all link variables on the lattice boundary are fixed to the identity, eliminating monopole world-line loops that wrap around the periodic directions. Simulations are performed for lattice sizes L=6 to 16, measuring the per-plaquette internal energy, specific heat, and Binder cumulant. The energy Binder cumulant and specific-heat peak behavior are analyzed to determine the order of the confinement-deconfinement transition. What is the ORDER of the finite-temperature confinement-deconfinement phase transition of compact U(1) lattice gauge theory as inferred from these fixed-boundary simulations? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: Compact U(1) lattice QED on 4D hypercubic lattice with fixed boundary conditions (identity links on boundary)
metadata.source_excerpt: Specific heat peak grows slowly and shifts toward beta~1, consistent with continuous transition... The combination of smooth energy variation under fixed-boundary conditions and the Binder cumulant tending to 2/3 imply periodic-boundary first-order signals are finite-volume topological artifacts
metadata.difficulty_note: Fixed-boundary (spherical topology) removes wrapped monopole artifacts that create spurious first-order signals in periodic simulations; result is continuous transition at ~beta=1.
